title: synth_lattice: Optionally do constant comparisons in soft logic
body:
Here we are adding a `-cmp2softlogic` option to `synth_lattice` to implement constant comparisons (arithmetic comparisons of a signal to a constant) in soft logic. Take e.g. the following:

```
read_verilog <<EOF
module top(input [15:0] a, output y);
	assign y = a >= 16'h7700;
endmodule
EOF
synth_lattice -family xo3 [-cmp2softlogic]
```

With the soft logic option, this takes up
```
   Number of cells:                  3
     LUT4                            3
```
while ordinarily it would take

```
   Number of cells:                 14
     CCU2D                           8
     LUT4                            6
```

The lower usage is due to two factors:

 * Once the comparisons are lowered to hard carry chains they won't be optimized, but here e.g. the lower 8 bits of the signal need not be compared at all, which is optimized away in the soft logic implementation. We could, and probaly should, extend `opt_expr` to optimize away the lower bits of the comparison on the `$ge` and other cells if the constant allows that, probably conditioned on the `-fine` mode. That would optimize the comparison before it gets implemented with the carry chains, so it would close the usage gap here somewhat.

* Optimized or not optimized, simple LUT implementation of the comparison takes up less resources anyway, by (I think) a factor of three. This is at the cost of higher delay since we are not using the carry paths.
